checks.a_analysis: The output does not contain any fabricated paragraphs or sentences. All body text and headings in the JSON have a direct basis in the source document. The document title in metadata and image alt text are expected AI-generated additions for accessibility.

The output does not contain any fabricated contact information such as phone numbers, emails, or URLs that do not exist in the source. While some existing URLs are missing from the output (which is checked in C6), no new or incorrect contact info was invented.
The conversion includes all major sections and both pages of the source document. No significant structural blocks of information were omitted in their entirety.
There are no tables in the source document to evaluate.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: While there are omissions of specific words and identifiers, these do not meet the 25% threshold for a 'significant text omission within a section' defined for this check. Each section starts and ends correctly and contains the vast majority of its intended text.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The output contains several omissions of functional identifiers that alter the meaning and utility of the document. Specifically, the link text for three major references (a statute section, a submission URL, and a policy name) is missing, leaving gaps in the sentences. These omissions mean a reader would not have the necessary information to comply with the memo's directives (e.g., they wouldn't know which website to visit to submit data).
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 1: 'under SL 2024-26, Section 7, and provides'; Page 1: 'website (it.nc.gov/805):'; Page 2: 'refer to the Acceptable Use Policy (AUP) published by'
checks.b_evidence_output: Page 1 (Reading Order 4 & 6): 'under ', ', and provides'; Page 1 (Reading Order 12 & 14): 'website (', '):'; Page 2 (Reading Order 32 & 34): 'refer to the ', ' published by'
checks.b_evidence_location: Sections: Introductory paragraph, Public Agency Reporting Requirements, and Page 2 Policy reference.

checks.a_analysis: The output JSON does not contain any duplicated paragraphs, sections, or list items. Each block of text from the source PDF appears only once in the output.

The heading hierarchy is logically structured. 'Memorandum' is an H2, followed by H3 subsections like 'State CIO Reporting Obligation'. Major directives on the second page return to H2 status. While H1 is reserved for the document title (Memo With Links 1334), there are no skips or illogical nesting.
The source document does not contain any tables, and neither does the output.

The logical flow of the document is preserved. Although several paragraphs are split into separate elements due to missing link text, and the logo (image) is moved from the top of the page to the end of the first section, the textual content follows the top-to-bottom order of the original PDF.

All lists in the document (the introductory list, reporting obligations list, reporting requirements list, and the exceptions list) have been correctly identified as unordered lists with the correct number of items.

There are multiple instances where links and their corresponding text are completely missing from the output. In several paragraphs, the conversion leaves an empty space where a link should be, often causing the paragraph to split into two elements.
checks.b_evidence_source: SL 2024-26, Section 7; it.nc.gov/805; Acceptable Use Policy (AUP)
checks.b_evidence_output: The text for these links is omitted entirely, e.g., in RO 32 and 34: 'Agencies may refer to the [missing text] published by NCDIT for guidance.'

checks.a_analysis: The visual organization of the document is faithfully preserved in the HTML version. The single-column layout correctly captures the memorandum structure and the subsequent sections. While the NCDIT logo has been moved from the top of the first page to a position following the 'Public Agency Reporting Requirements' section (corresponding to the original page break), the flow of information remains clear and logical. The headers, sub-headers, and bulleted lists are presented in the same order as the source document, ensuring the user can find information in the expected sequence.
The source document contains no tables. This check is not applicable, but as no corrupted table structures are present in the output, it passes.

The primary meaningful image in the source document is the NCDIT logo/banner. This image is present and correctly rendered in the HTML output. Although its vertical position has shifted due to the conversion process, it is fully visible and functionally equivalent to the original branding. No other images or diagrams are present in the source.

There is no visual duplication of body content. The document headings appear in the navigation Table of Contents and correctly once in the main body, which is a standard accessible design practice and not a defect. No paragraphs, tables, or signature blocks are repeated within the document flow.
